mission.title: harbor: Can not delete user login with oidc
mission.description: Can not delete user login with oidc. Requested by 31+ users.

steps.title: Check current harbor deployment
steps.description:
Verify your harbor version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n harbor -l app.kubernetes.io/name=harbor
helm list -n harbor 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working harbor installation.

steps.title: Review harbor OIDC configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant harbor configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n harbor -l app.kubernetes.io/name=harbor
kubectl get configmap -n harbor -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=harbor
```
When using an OIDC provider like Dex or Keycloak, deleted users in the provider cannot be removed through the Harbor dashboard.

steps.title: Delete the OIDC user from Harbor's database
steps.description:
Connect to the Harbor database pod and remove the user records directly:
```bash
# Get the database pod name
POD_NAME=$(kubectl get pods -n harbor --output=json | jq -r '.items[].metadata | select(.name | startswith("harbor-database")) | .name')

# Open a shell in the database pod
kubectl exec -it -n harbor $POD_NAME -- bash

# Connect to the PostgreSQL CLI
psql -U postgres
```
Then run the following SQL to remove the user:
```sql
\c registry

-- Find the user_id for the user to delete
SELECT user_id FROM harbor_user WHERE username = 'target_username';

-- Replace <USER_ID> with the actual user_id from the query above
DELETE FROM project_member WHERE entity_id = <USER_ID> AND entity_type = 'u';
DELETE FROM oidc_user WHERE user_id = <USER_ID>;
DELETE FROM harbor_user WHERE user_id = <USER_ID>;
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n harbor -l app.kubernetes.io/name=harbor
kubectl get events -n harbor --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Can not delete user login with oidc" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: When OIDC authentication is enabled in Harbor and a user is removed from the OIDC provider (e.g., Dex or Keycloak), the user cannot log in via the UI but Docker CLI access may persist. Harbor does not expose a UI option to delete OIDC-authenticated users. The workaround is to directly delete the user records from Harbor's PostgreSQL database by removing rows from the oidc_user, project_member, and harbor_user tables for the target user_id.
codeSnippets:
# Get the database pod name
POD_NAME=$(kubectl get pods -n harbor --output=json | jq -r '.items[].metadata | select(.name | startswith("harbor-database")) | .name')

# Open a shell in the database pod
kubectl exec -it -n harbor $POD_NAME -- bash

# Connect to the PostgreSQL CLI
psql -U postgres

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with harbor installed or the issue environment reproducible.
